title: U.S. tech layoffs hit near two-year high in May as AI accounts for 40% of job cuts
description: U.S. tech companies cut 38,242 jobs in May 2026, the most in nearly two years, with AI cited as the cause in 40% of cases. That's up from just 7% in January.

title: You Can Get This AI Podcast Creator for A$139 Right Now
description: If you’ve wanted to start a podcast but don’t want to spend weekends editing audio, this is ideal. Get lifetime access to the PoddyHost AI Podcast Generator Starter Plan for a one-time A$139 (MSRP A$758), though prices can change at any time.

title: How AI governance is taking shape in the Philippines
description: As Congress tackles the rapid rise of artificial intelligence, a slew of proposed bills aim to establish regulatory frameworks, protect workers, and ensure ethical standards in AI development and deployment

title: Inside the British lab hunting for dangers lurking in AI
description: The government's AI Security Institute, staffed by alumni from OpenAI and Google, is becoming a model for countries grappling with AI's emerging risks.

title: US government could take stakes in major AI companies
description: U.S. officials are weighing a proposal that could see the federal government take stakes in leading artificial intelligence providers. The discussions were first reported by news outlet NOTUS on Thursday. According to Politico, President Donald Trump confirmed the report today during a press briefing aboard Air Force One. Trump stated that he will likely meet [...] The post US government could take stakes in major AI companies appeared first on SiliconANGLE .
